description: S3 bucket security issues including public access, path traversal, and access control weaknesses.

entries.name: S3 Bucket Without Block Public Access
entries.description: S3 bucket missing BlockPublicAccess settings, allowing bucket policies or ACLs to grant public access.
entries.how_it_works: Without S3 Block Public Access, a misconfigured bucket policy (Principal: '*') or ACL (public-read) makes objects accessible to anyone on the internet.
preconditions: Bucket policy or ACL allows public access
impact: Public exposure of all objects in bucket
entries.detection_guidance: Check CDK: block_public_access=s3.BlockPublicAccess.BLOCK_ALL must be present. Check all 4 settings: BlockPublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, IgnorePublicAcls, RestrictPublicBuckets.
remediation: Add block_public_access=BlockPublicAccess.BLOCK_ALL
Enable enforce_ssl=True
Apply at account level via S3 Account Public Access Block

entries.name: S3 Key Path Traversal via Presigned URL
entries.description: User-controlled filename in presigned URL key allows accessing objects outside intended prefix.
entries.how_it_works: Application generates presigned URLs using filenames from user input. If the filename contains path separators or isn't validated against an expected prefix, the user can generate URLs for objects belonging to other tenants.
preconditions: Presigned URL key includes user-controlled filename
No path normalization or prefix validation
affected_components: Any S3 PUT/GET with user-controlled key
entries.detection_guidance: Find generate_presigned_url or put_object where the Key parameter includes data from request body. Check if os.path.basename() or prefix validation is applied.
entries.vulnerable_code:
filename = body['filename']
key = f'{customer_id}/{filename}'
url = s3.generate_presigned_url('put_object', Params={'Bucket': B, 'Key': key})
entries.safe_code:
filename = os.path.basename(body['filename'])[:255]  # Strip path
assert filename  # Not empty after stripping
upload_id = str(uuid4())
key = f'{auth_tenant_id}/evidence/{upload_id}/{filename}'
assert key.startswith(f'{auth_tenant_id}/')  # Verify prefix
url = s3.generate_presigned_url('put_object', Params={'Bucket': B, 'Key': key})
remediation: Use os.path.basename() to strip path components
Add UUID prefix to prevent predictability
Validate final key starts with authenticated tenant prefix
Use tenant_id from auth context, never request body

entries.name: S3 Bucket Without Encryption
entries.description: S3 bucket stores data without server-side encryption at rest.
entries.how_it_works: Objects stored without encryption are readable if bucket access is compromised. For compliance data, regulations may require encryption.
impact: Data readable without decryption if access control is bypassed
entries.detection_guidance: Check for encryption property in bucket definition. S3_MANAGED is minimum, KMS with CMK is preferred for compliance.
exploit_payloads: N/A — this is a configuration weakness, not directly exploitable without access
remediation: Enable SSE-S3 (minimum) or SSE-KMS (recommended for compliance)
Enable bucket key to reduce KMS API costs
For HIPAA: use CMK with rotation enabled

entries.name: S3 Bucket Without Access Logging
entries.description: No server access logging on S3 bucket means who accessed which objects is unauditable.
entries.how_it_works: Without access logging, there's no record of GET/PUT/DELETE operations on objects. If data is stolen or tampered, there's no forensic trail.
impact: Cannot detect unauthorized access after the fact
entries.detection_guidance: Check for server_access_logs_bucket property. For compliance platforms storing evidence, this is mandatory.
exploit_payloads: N/A — absence of logging means attacks go undetected
remediation: Enable server access logging to a separate bucket
Or use CloudTrail S3 data events (more structured, costlier)
Set lifecycle policy on log bucket (retain 1+ years for compliance)

entries.name: S3 Bucket Without Versioning (Evidence Tampering)
entries.description: Without versioning, objects can be overwritten or deleted with no recovery. Critical for compliance evidence integrity.
entries.how_it_works: If an attacker (or compromised Lambda) overwrites a compliance evidence file, the original is permanently lost. There's no way to prove what the original content was.
preconditions: Bucket contains compliance evidence or audit data
Any principal with write access can overwrite
entries.detection_guidance: Check for versioned=True on buckets storing evidence, evaluations, or audit data.
exploit_payloads: aws s3 cp malicious-evidence.pdf s3://bucket/tenant-a/evidence/eval-123.pdf  # Overwrites original
entries.safe_code:
s3.Bucket(self, 'Evidence',
    versioned=True,
    # Optional: prevent even deletion of versions
    # object_lock_enabled=True (for immutable compliance records)
)
remediation: Enable versioning (versioned=True)
Consider Object Lock for immutable compliance records
Set lifecycle rules to transition old versions to Glacier
Restrict s3:DeleteObjectVersion permission

entries.name: S3 Bucket Policy Allows Cross-Account Access
entries.description: Bucket policy grants access to external AWS accounts without condition constraints.
entries.how_it_works: A bucket policy with Principal: 'arn:aws:iam::OTHER_ACCOUNT:root' allows that account full access. If the external account is compromised, your data is compromised.
preconditions: Bucket policy specifies external account principal
No conditions restricting the access (e.g., aws:SourceOrg, VPC endpoint)
impact: External account can read/write bucket objects
Data exfiltration by compromised partner account
entries.detection_guidance: Search bucket policies for Principal with external account IDs. Verify conditions are present.
exploit_payloads: From external account: aws s3 ls s3://target-bucket --profile external
remediation: Scope resources to specific prefixes
Add aws:PrincipalOrgID condition
Add VPC endpoint condition if applicable
Review regularly — external access should have expiry
